title: The 18 Best Restaurants in Buffalo
author: Jessica Kelly
summary: Buffalo may be best known for its hot wings, thanks to the masterminds at Anchor Bar, but the city’s food scene goes far beyond a single bar app. It especially excels at unpretentious comfort foods, like Buffalo style pizza — pillowy, doughy crust with cup-and-char pepperoni (aka “grease pools”) — beef on weck: perfectly cooked, slow roasted beef on a kummelweck roll (coated generously with whole caraway seeds and salt), served with a side of pickles and horseradish (ideally from local vendor Broadway Market). Locals also celebrate the city’s cultural diversity through food, including the large Polish community, which serves some of the
full_text:
Open for: Lunch, early dinner

Price range: $$

Potts Deli has a second standalone location on Rossler Avenue, but try the one inside Buffalo’s famous Broadway Market. It’s one of those “if you know, you know” type places that will make you feel like an instant local. In the market’s back corner, near the deli counter, this Polish specialist serves pierogies stuffed with sauerkraut or farmers cheese, Polish sausage, and czarnina (a sweet-sour soup of broth, dried fruits, vinegar, and blood, traditionally from poultry or pigs). In the springtime, leading up to Easter, Broadway Market brings in vendors from around the city, making it a great time to visit.

Know before you go: For the quickest meal, bring a friend and plan to divide and conquer. One person can order at the deli, while the other saves a spot in line.
